title: Arithmetic Applications of Random Multiplicative Functions
speaker: Max Wenqiang Xu
abstract: I will give an overview of recent progress in random multiplicative functions (random models for multiplicative functions) and their connection to the study of the Fyodorov-Hiary-Keating conjecture and Polya's question on nonnegative character sums; and applications to character sums estimates and Mobius cancellations.

proof_stages.label: Steinhaus RMF model and Harper cancellation
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Steinhaus random multiplicative functions by iid unit-circle values on primes, motivates them as models for typical characters or n^{it}, and states Harper's theorem giving cancellation of order sqrt(x)/(log log x)^{1/4}.

proof_stages.label: Transference to deterministic character families
proof_stages.summary: A first arithmetic application replaces the random function by Dirichlet characters modulo q, or by n^{it}, and obtains the same averaged partial-sum bound by reducing the deterministic family to the RMF theorem.

proof_stages.label: Twisted character sums and Möbius consequences
proof_stages.summary: The second application uses characters as auxiliary twists of a multiplicative coefficient a_n, formulates Harper's conjectural removal of the conductor-range restriction, and explains how this would imply strong Möbius cancellation in short intervals and progressions.

proof_stages.label: Random Euler products and critical GMC mechanism
proof_stages.summary: The proof mechanism rewrites RMF partial sums through a random Euler product, views its logarithm as a Gaussian-like field, and identifies the relevant |F|^2 integral as critical Gaussian multiplicative chaos whose renormalization supplies the log-log saving.

proof_stages.label: Log-correlated fields and zeta local maxima
proof_stages.summary: The same covariance structure is presented as log-correlated for RMF Euler products and for log zeta, leading to the Fyodorov-Keating conjecture for local maxima and explaining the correlation-dependent second-order term.

proof_stages.label: Polya positive-definite characters via local maxima
proof_stages.summary: Control of local maxima in L-function families is then applied to Polya's problem, yielding bounds on the rarity of positive-definite quadratic characters and suggesting the expected logarithmic-power asymptotic.

proof_stages.label: Distributional laws for weighted RMF sums
proof_stages.summary: Recent work shifts from bounding sizes to determining limiting distributions of weighted sums sum a_n f(n), showing Gaussian limits for weights that destroy multiplicative chaos and Gaussian-times-random-factor limits for more multiplicative weights.

proof_stages.label: Short-interval RMF laws and variance transition
proof_stages.summary: For short intervals x<n≤x+y, the final result gives Gaussian limits after a variance B_{x,y} that interpolates between iid-like square-root-y scaling and the global GMC/log-log normalization.
